description: PyTorch binary image classification — custom CatDogCNN (150x150, 4-block CNN, CrossEntropyLoss) with GPU training, Gradio web UI, and Google Colab notebook

tech_stack.dataset: Oxford-IIIT Pet Dataset (public, auto-download via torchvision)

results.requirement: Custom layers only, no pretrained shortcuts

results.note: Fixed — now generated by train.py automatically

results.requirement: Loss tracked and plotted per epoch
User can upload/load image via UI

bugs_fixed.description: nn.Softmax inside model + CrossEntropyLoss = double-softmax (numerically incorrect loss)
bugs_fixed.fix: Removed nn.Softmax from model.py classifier. Added torch.softmax() in predict.py, app.py, visualize.py at inference time.

bugs_fixed.description: dataset.py returned float32 labels but CrossEntropyLoss requires torch.long
bugs_fixed.fix: Changed OxfordPetBinaryDataset.__getitem__ to return torch.long. Removed redundant .long() cast in train.py.

bugs_fixed.description: Confusion matrix only existed in visualize.py (optional manual step), not in train.py
bugs_fixed.fix: Added confusion matrix generation at end of train.py main() using best checkpoint. Saves to output/confusion_matrix.png automatically.

tasks.title: Environment Setup
steps: Verify GPU with: python -c "import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available())"
acceptance_criteria: torch.cuda.is_available() returns True on GPU machine
All packages import without error

tasks.title: Dataset Preparation
tasks.description: Oxford-IIIT Pet Dataset auto-downloaded via torchvision. Supports full dataset (80/20 split) or balanced sampling (--train-per-class N --val-samples N).
steps: Dataset downloads automatically on first training run (~750 MB)
Binary labels: Cat=0, Dog=1 as torch.long (required by CrossEntropyLoss)
acceptance_criteria: OxfordIIITPet downloads without error
DataLoader returns (B, 3, 150, 150) tensors with torch.long labels 0 or 1
Default split: 80/20 of 3,680 trainval images (2,944 train / 736 val)
Balanced split: python train.py --train-per-class 2000 --val-samples 2000

tasks.title: CatDogCNN Architecture
tasks.description: Custom 4-block CNN for binary classification — no pretrained weights

architecture.head: Flatten → Linear(6272→512) + ReLU → Linear(512→2)
architecture.output: (B, 2) raw logits — apply torch.softmax(dim=1) at inference
architecture.loss: nn.CrossEntropyLoss (expects raw logits, applies log_softmax internally)

acceptance_criteria: python model.py → output=torch.Size([4, 2])  trainable_params=3,453,634
No nn.Softmax inside model (CrossEntropyLoss requires raw logits)
build_model() factory returns CatDogCNN() with no arguments

tasks.title: GPU Training Script
tasks.description: Train CatDogCNN on GPU with full training loop, AMP, confusion matrix auto-generated after training
steps: Monitor loss and accuracy each epoch
Confusion matrix auto-generated to ./output/confusion_matrix.png after training

cli_options.--train-per-class: integer (0 = full dataset; e.g. 2000 = balanced 2000 cats + 2000 dogs)
cli_options.--val-samples: integer (0 = full remainder; e.g. 2000 = 2000 val images)

acceptance_criteria: Training loop runs without error on CUDA GPU
output/best_model.pth created
output/training_curves.png generated
output/confusion_matrix.png generated automatically at end of training

tasks.title: Single Image Prediction (CLI)
tasks.description: Run inference on any cat or dog image via command line
steps: Run: python predict.py --image path/to/image.jpg --chart  (saves confidence chart)
Run: python predict.py --upload  (native file browser dialog)
acceptance_criteria: predict.py loads checkpoint without error
Output: label (Cat/Dog) + confidence % + per-class probabilities
torch.softmax() applied to raw logits before reporting probabilities
Works with JPEG and PNG files

tasks.title: Gradio Web UI
tasks.description: Browser-based image upload and prediction with confidence histogram
steps: Upload any cat or dog image
acceptance_criteria: Image upload widget renders in browser
Prediction triggers automatically on image change
gr.Label shows Cat/Dog probabilities
Matplotlib bar chart shows P(Cat) and P(Dog) in %
torch.softmax() applied to raw logits before display

tasks.title: Google Colab Jupyter Notebook
tasks.description: End-to-end notebook for Google Colab with free T4 GPU — needs update for CatDogCNN architecture
acceptance_criteria: All cells run without error on Colab T4 GPU
Dataset downloads automatically
Model trains CatDogCNN (not ResNet-18)
Uses CrossEntropyLoss with raw logits (no Softmax in model)
Inference cell applies torch.softmax() at output
tasks.note: Notebook still references old ResNet-18 architecture — needs update to CatDogCNN

tasks.title: Post-Training Visualization Suite
tasks.description: Run visualize.py after training for enhanced plots: dark-theme curves, 3x3 sample grid, confidence histogram, per-class accuracy bars
outputs: training_curves_enhanced.png — dark-theme loss & accuracy curves with best-epoch marker
confusion_matrix.png — 2x2 TP/TN/FP/FN heatmap (also auto-generated by train.py)
confidence_distribution.png — P(Dog) histogram by true class
acceptance_criteria: All 5 PNG files generated without error
torch.softmax() applied to raw logits in collect_predictions()
Sample grid is 3x3 (9 images)

tasks.title: Update Colab Notebook for CatDogCNN
tasks.description: CatsAndDogs_Colab.ipynb still references the old ResNet-18 architecture. Update it to use CatDogCNN with CrossEntropyLoss, 150x150 input, and raw logits at output.
steps: Replace ResNet-18 model cell with CatDogCNN definition (copy from model.py)
Update inference cell to apply torch.softmax(output, dim=1) before reading probabilities
Update build_model() call to remove architecture argument
Test all cells run end-to-end on Colab T4 GPU
acceptance_criteria: Notebook runs end-to-end without error
Model architecture matches model.py exactly
Inference produces correct Cat/Dog probabilities

tasks.title: Hyperparameter Tuning
tasks.description: Improve accuracy beyond the current ~83% baseline with the CatDogCNN architecture
suggestions: Add BatchNorm after each Conv layer
Add Dropout(0.5) before the final Linear
Increase image size to 224x224 (update Linear head accordingly: 128*7*7 no longer applies)
Try a deeper classifier head: Linear(6272→1024→256→2)
acceptance_criteria: Validation accuracy > 90% on full Oxford dataset

milestones.name: Core Pipeline Working
milestones.deliverable: Model trains on GPU, confusion matrix auto-generated

milestones.name: Inference & UI Complete
milestones.deliverable: CLI prediction + Gradio web UI both working

milestones.name: Colab Notebook Updated
milestones.deliverable: Notebook runs end-to-end with CatDogCNN architecture
